data_in.description: GNPS public library spectra in matchms format

data_out.description: Subset of spectra with newly derived SMILES, InChI, and InChIKey annotations where monoisotopic mass validation passed
Change log documenting compound names, newly added annotations, parent mass match validation, and error cases

domain_knowledge: Monoisotopic mass (exact mass calculated from exact atomic masses) differs from molar mass and must be computed from SMILES structure to validate parent mass assignments in MS/MS spectra.
PubChem API returns canonical SMILES and molecular formulas for compound names; multiple isomers may exist for a single name, requiring strict mass-based filtering to select the correct structure.

The GNPS library contains predominantly compound names without structure annotations; deriving SMILES from PubChem significantly expands machine-readable annotation coverage for downstream spectral matching.

Parent mass in MS/MS spectra refers to the monoisotopic mass of the molecular ion [M+H]⁺ or [M-H]⁻ (depending on ionization mode); validation must account for the ionization mode and assigned adduct to correctly calculate expected parent mass from neutral molecular mass.

direct_checks: Verify file exists at zenodo.org/10.5281/zenodo.10160791 containing cleaned GNPS library and filter settings
Verify file exists at github.com/matchms/matchms containing matchms source code with 'derive annotation from compound name' filter implementation

Script runs without error: load matchms library, apply only the 'derive annotation from compound name' filter to spectra with compound name but no SMILES, query PubChem for each, validate returned monoisotopic mass against stored parent mass, output filtered spectrum subset
Output file format is valid structured spectrum record (MGF, JSON, or pickle format matching matchms Spectrum object serialization)

Output contains only spectra that: (a) had compound name in input, (b) lacked SMILES before filter, (c) received PubChem lookup result, (d) monoisotopic mass of PubChem result matched stored parent mass (within tolerance—parameter-sensitive, requires specification of mass tolerance threshold)
Field 'SMILES' present in all output spectra records
Field 'compound_name' present in all output spectra records
For subset of output spectra, byte-for-byte compare derived SMILES against reference PubChem canonical SMILES to detect any transformation errors
expert_review: Chemist or metabolomics expert review: for random sample of output spectra, verify that PubChem-derived SMILES structures are chemically reasonable and correspond to the original compound names
Expert assessment: confirm that mass tolerance threshold chosen for monoisotopic mass validation is appropriate for the mass spectrometry instrument resolution typical in GNPS library acquisitions
Expert assessment: verify that the filter correctly handles edge cases such as isotope-labeled compounds, salt forms, and ambiguous compound names that may retrieve multiple PubChem hits

task_description: Apply the 'derive annotation from compound name' filter to GNPS library spectra lacking SMILES annotations, querying PubChem for each compound name and accepting only results where the returned monoisotopic mass matches the stored parent mass; output the subset of spectra that received new SMILES, InChI, and InChIKey annotations.

task_inputs.label: GNPS public library spectra in matchms format

task_objective: Apply PubChem-based SMILES derivation to spectra lacking SMILES but containing compound names, validate hits by parent mass matching, and output the subset of spectra that received new valid annotations.

task_outputs.label: Spectra with newly derived SMILES/InChI/InChIKey where mass validation passed
Change log with compound names, new annotations, and validation results

title: Filter: derive SMILES/InChI/InChIKey annotation from compound name via PubChem lookup with parent mass validation
uncertainty_notes: This card was generated by the LLM-assisted pipeline and needs scientific expert review.
Each TracedClaim's evidence_span has been substring-checked against its source section; see logs/llm_calls.jsonl and capsules/<task_id>/quality_report.json for groundedness results.
workflow_description: 1. Load GNPS library spectra filtered to those with a compound name field but no existing SMILES/InChI/InChIKey using matchms. 2. For each spectrum, query PubChem using the compound name to retrieve canonical SMILES, InChI, and InChIKey. 3. Calculate the monoisotopic mass from the returned SMILES using RDKit. 4. Retain the PubChem-derived annotation only if the calculated monoisotopic mass matches the spectrum's stored parent mass within tolerance. 5. Write the annotated spectra to output, recording metadata changes (original compound name, new SMILES, new InChI, new InChIKey, parent mass match status) in a change log.
